task_completion.description: Access the Item management interface in NCH Copper Point of Sale.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 'Items' list window or a 'New Item' dialog box open within the NCH Copper Point of Sale application.

task_completion.description: Create the 'Virus Removal' item with correct code and price.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'New Item' dialog or the item list shows an item with Description 'Virus Removal', Item Code 'VR01', and Price '$89.00'.
In the item settings for 'Virus Removal', the checkbox for inventory tracking, stock management, or quantity tracking is visibly unchecked or disabled.

task_completion.description: Create the 'System Tune-up' item with correct code and price.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'New Item' dialog or the item list shows an item with Description 'System Tune-up', Item Code 'ST01', and Price '$49.99'.
In the item settings for 'System Tune-up', the checkbox for inventory tracking, stock management, or quantity tracking is visibly unchecked or disabled.

task_completion.description: Create the 'Data Transfer' item with correct code and price.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'New Item' dialog or the item list shows an item with Description 'Data Transfer', Item Code 'DT01', and Price '$60.00'.
In the item settings for 'Data Transfer', the checkbox for inventory tracking, stock management, or quantity tracking is visibly unchecked or disabled.

integrity.description: The agent used the NCH Copper Point of Sale graphical user interface to create the items.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the actual NCH Copper POS software windows and dialogs, rather than a database viewer (like DB Browser for SQLite).

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows or code editors are shown executing SQL INSERT commands or Python scripts to modify the SQLite database directly.

integrity.description: The item details were entered into the correct application fields.
integrity.visual_evidence: The item codes, descriptions, and prices are visible inside the specific input fields of the Copper POS 'Item' dialog, not just written in a text editor or notepad.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses NCH Copper Point of Sale v3.06. You must verify that three specific service items (Virus Removal, System Tune-up, Data Transfer) are created with their exact codes (VR01, ST01, DT01) and prices ($89.00, $49.99, $60.00). Crucially, check that inventory tracking / stock management is disabled for each of these items, as they are services. Note: The programmatic verifier for this task has a known critical bug (a NameError on line 63 of verifier.py), meaning visual verification of the GUI is the only reliable source of truth for task success.
